message:
chore(beads): sync beads issues.jsonl

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -487,7 +487,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","title":"[quality] Build the placeholder inventory, ownership map, and regression gates","description":"Build the authoritative inventory and the mechanical guardrails for this effort. This epic exists to stop the work from degrading into anecdotal cleanup or string replacement. It should turn the audit into a machine-checkable inventory, define which paths are allowed to remain test-only/template-only scaffolds, and add automated scans so future contributors cannot accidentally reintroduce production placeholders.","acceptance_criteria":"- The audited inventory is complete, categorized, and mapped to concrete closure strategies and approved exceptions.
- Automated scanning fails on banned placeholder patterns in production or runtime code and emits explainable logs for allowlisted cases.
- The epic leaves behind unit-test coverage for inventory and scanner rules plus end-to-end scan scripts and artifacts that the final closure review can consume directly.","notes":"Allowed exception classes should be intentionally narrow: tests, fixtures, mock infrastructure such as fcp-testkit, documented code generators that are themselves covered by regression tests, and the shared evidence-bundle convention created in 1.4. Runtime connectors, manifests, CLI/operator commands, and shared crates should not rely on ad hoc allowlists. This epic now owns not just the inventory and scanner but also the stable proof layout the closure audit will consume.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:00.562924Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:00:28.960271Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholders","quality","tooling","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:00.562924Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2294,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","author":"Codex","text":"Use this epic to make placeholder regressions mechanically expensive. The exit condition is not just a clean grep; it is a trustworthy allowlist plus automated enforcement that future sessions can rely on.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:01Z"},{"id":2302,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","author":"Codex","text":"Operational note: treat this bead as a coordination/closure node, not the default claim target. Prefer claiming its concrete leaf tasks unless the work is explicitly cross-cutting orchestration across multiple child beads.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:40:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.1","title":"[quality] Lock the audited production-placeholder inventory and approved exception list","description":"Convert the audit into an explicit machine-checkable inventory. Capture each high-confidence placeholder with its file location, why it is incomplete, whether it is a runtime blocker or an allowed scaffold candidate, the intended exit strategy, and the owner epic/task that will clear it. The output should make it impossible to lose track of a placeholder just because the wording changes.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every audited placeholder is represented with file or surface, rationale, target fix or quarantine strategy, owner, and verification expectations.
- Approved exception classes are explicit and small enough for automation to enforce.
- The inventory ships with classifier unit tests and a repo-wide scan artifact and log that later scanner and closure beads can consume without re-running manual archaeology.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberForge","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:31.280641Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:56:41.685988Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:56:41.685748Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["inventory","placeholders","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:31.280641Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2309,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.1","author":"Codex","text":"Audit anchors: the full suspect set listed on the initiative, spanning connectors/tlon, connectors/zalo, connectors/zalouser, connectors/huggingface, connectors/aws, connectors/s3, connectors/gcp, crates/fcp-bootstrap, crates/fcp-core, crates/fcp-mesh, crates/fwc, and connector limit/generator files. Verification contract: unit-test the inventory classifier/allowlist logic, emit a machine-readable inventory artifact, and run a repo-wide end-to-end smoke over the scan inputs with detailed logs showing which findings were classified as runtime blockers versus allowed scaffolds.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:40:21Z"},{"id":2341,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.1","author":"AmberForge","text":"Claimed and moving. Locked a machine-checkable placeholder inventory at docs/testing/placeholder-inventory.json, added scripts/ci/placeholder_inventory_scan.sh, and generated docs/testing/placeholder-scan.json plus docs/testing/placeholder-scan.log against the current tree. Notable drift from the earlier audit: crates/fwc/src/audit_chain/mod.rs no longer exposes audit.tail.not_implemented in this worktree, so I removed that stale anchor from the locked inventory rather than preserving outdated archaeology. Agent Mail write-path remains blocked by the known storage.sqlite3 corruption bead, so coordination awareness is still archive-read fallback while verification runs.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:28:59Z"},{"id":2344,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.1","author":"AmberForge","text":"Verification summary: `rch exec -- cargo test -p fwc placeholder_inventory -- --nocapture` passed with the new inventory tests green. `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` completed successfully on the remote worker, so the inventory/scan work did not introduce compile failures. `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` still reports broad pre-existing formatting drift across unrelated connectors/crates; I only corrected the one rustfmt-requested wrap in `crates/fwc/src/audit.rs` rather than reformatting unrelated work. `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` is currently blocked before linting by an existing workspace manifest problem in `connectors/gcp/Cargo.toml` (`dependency.rsa` missing from workspace.dependencies), so there is no bead-local clippy signal to act on yet.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:56:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","title":"[quality] Add automated scanning that fails on production placeholders outside the allowlist","description":"Add automated scanning for banned placeholder signals in production/runtime code and manifest/runtime status drift. The scanner should distinguish allowed test/template paths from real shipping surfaces and catch the specific audited patterns, including planned_only/live mismatches, placeholder bench outputs, fake auth markers, and generated runtime code that still emits TODO/placeholder operations.","acceptance_criteria":"- Production or runtime scans fail on audited placeholder classes outside the approved allowlist and explain every failure or allowlisted exemption in detailed logs.
- Manifest or runtime status drift is checked automatically.
- Unit tests cover banned-pattern and allowlist rules, and end-to-end scripts cover seeded fixtures plus a full-repo scan recipe suitable for local runs and CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberForge","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:31.789073Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:34:29.794841Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T03:34:29.794603Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholders","quality","tooling","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:31.789073Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:12.012911Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2310,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","author":"Codex","text":"Audit anchors: production Rust under connectors/**/src and crates/**/src, connector manifest.toml files, and generated connector output from crates/fwc/src/new_cmd.rs. Verification contract: add unit tests for each banned pattern/allowlist rule plus end-to-end scan scripts that exercise seeded fixtures and a full-repo run, with detailed logs explaining every failure and every allowlisted path.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:40:22Z"},{"id":2345,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","author":"AmberForge","text":"Claimed after completing flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.1. Live MCP Agent Mail is still blocked by the storage.sqlite3 database error, so coordination visibility remains archive-read fallback. Starting with the inventory/scan surfaces I just landed in crates/fwc/src/audit.rs and scripts/ci/placeholder_inventory_scan.sh, then I will wire enforcement/tests so production/runtime scans fail on non-allowlisted placeholders and status drift.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:57:15Z"},{"id":2349,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","author":"AmberForge","text":"Scanner work is in place and I tightened the Rust-side contract after the first rch test run exposed a coupling bug: load_placeholder_inventory was doing full workspace-anchor validation, which made cargo test fail on known tracked inventory drift (currently the resolved GCP service-account gap still listed in the inventory). I split structural validation from explicit workspace-anchor validation and replaced the repo-coupled unit test with synthetic validator coverage so the scanner reports drift while the unit suite stays green on the intended contract. Waiting on the follow-up rch placeholder test slice now.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:24:49Z"},{"id":2351,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","author":"AmberForge","text":"Completed implementation and verification. Added production-placeholder inventory helpers/tests in crates/fwc/src/audit.rs, refreshed scripts/ci/placeholder_inventory_scan.sh so it supports full-repo and fixture-root runs, and added scripts/e2e/placeholder_inventory_scan_fixture.sh to seed deterministic known-gap / approved-exception / unexpected-spread cases. Verification: rch exec -- cargo test -p fwc placeholder_ -- --nocapture passed; bash scripts/e2e/placeholder_inventory_scan_fixture.sh passed; scripts/ci/placeholder_inventory_scan.sh --json-out docs/testing/placeholder-scan.json --log-out docs/testing/placeholder-scan.log exited 1 as intended against the live repo and now reports 13 findings with 3 inventory drifts, 9 known-gap blockers, and 1 unexpected-match finding. Live MCP Agent Mail remains unavailable due the storage.sqlite3 database error, so coordination evidence is still bead comments plus archive fallback.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:34:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","title":"[quality] Run the final closure audit and exit review for placeholder eradication","description":"After all implementation slices land, rerun the placeholder inventory, validate the scanner, and publish the closure review for this initiative. This bead is the anti-regression exit gate: it should confirm that audited placeholders are genuinely gone or explicitly quarantined, with links to tests and artifacts rather than prose-only confidence.","acceptance_criteria":"- No unresolved audited production placeholder remains without an explicit tracked rationale.
- The closure bundle includes final scanner output, unit, integration, and end-to-end references, transcripts or logs, and a pass or fail table for every original audited placeholder family including incubating or quarantined exceptions.
- Closure criteria distinguish real implementation from renamed or hidden placeholder behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:32.293909Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:40.822075Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholders","quality","review","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:32.293909Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:12.266795Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:00:27.873379Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:12.510561Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:12.753567Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.000070Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.244962Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:48:31.510537Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.490537Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.735810Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.993667Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:14.250390Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:14.497816Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:14.750153Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:15.007051Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-mmvqb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:40.425272Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:40.821547Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2311,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","author":"Codex","text":"Closure evidence must consume the outputs of the scanner, the per-domain verification suites, and the end-to-end scripts created under the child beads. Verification contract: attach the final scan output, test references, transcript/log bundles, and an explicit pass/fail table for each original audited placeholder so future reviewers do not need to reconstruct the audit by hand.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:40:22Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","title":"[quality] Run the final closure audit and exit review for placeholder eradication","description":"After all implementation slices land, rerun the placeholder inventory, validate the scanner, and publish the closure review for this initiative. This bead is the anti-regression exit gate: it should confirm that audited placeholders are genuinely gone or explicitly quarantined, with links to tests and artifacts rather than prose-only confidence.","acceptance_criteria":"- No unresolved audited production placeholder remains without an explicit tracked rationale.
- The closure bundle includes final scanner output, unit, integration, and end-to-end references, transcripts or logs, and a pass or fail table for every original audited placeholder family including incubating or quarantined exceptions.
- Closure criteria distinguish real implementation from renamed or hidden placeholder behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:32.293909Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:30:58.010078Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholders","quality","review","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:32.293909Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:12.266795Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:00:27.873379Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:12.510561Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:12.753567Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.000070Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.244962Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:48:31.510537Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.490537Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.735810Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:13.993667Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:14.250390Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:14.497816Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:14.750153Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:15.007051Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-mmvqb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:40.425272Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:40.821547Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2311,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","author":"Codex","text":"Closure evidence must consume the outputs of the scanner, the per-domain verification suites, and the end-to-end scripts created under the child beads. Verification contract: attach the final scan output, test references, transcript/log bundles, and an explicit pass/fail table for each original audited placeholder so future reviewers do not need to reconstruct the audit by hand.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:40:22Z"},{"id":2739,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-18 pre-closure evidence gathering:

1. All 5 placeholder scanner tests pass: generated_scaffolds_pass_placeholder_inventory_scan, generated_scaffolds_reject_rust_placeholder_macros, generated_scaffolds_reject_dead_end_comments_in_runtime, generated_scaffolds_cap_instructional_todo_count, generated_manifests_reject_placeholder_operations

2. Broad repo grep confirms zero todo!/unimplemented! in production or connector code (0 matches across connectors/, 0 in crates/ outside scanner patterns)

3. planned_only/placeholder_operation matches are all legitimate:
   - firecrawl: conditional runtime state (live vs incubating per 24llg.2.6)
   - tlon/zalo/zalouser: test function names only
   - fcp-sdk migration.rs: test fixture TOML
   - fwc readiness.rs: test asserting tag string stability
   - fwc new_cmd.rs: scanner pattern definitions

4. Remaining blocker for this bead: r4x01 (rch retrieval overflow — upstream tooling, not repo code)

5. All other 16 dependency beads are CLOSED.","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:30:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","title":"[quality] Define a shared evidence-bundle and e2e verification convention for placeholder eradication","description":"Define one reusable evidence contract for this initiative so unit tests, integration coverage, end-to-end scripts, logs, transcripts, and artifact bundles do not sprawl into ad hoc per-bead conventions. This bead should specify where verification scripts live, how structured logs and snapshots are named, how secret-safe redaction works, how reruns are invoked locally and in CI, and how the final closure audit consumes the resulting artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"- The initiative has a documented and machine-usable convention for verification script locations, artifact naming, transcript capture, structured logging fields, and secret-safe redaction.
- The convention covers unit, integration, and end-to-end layers, includes predictable local and CI rerun commands, and uses the crate-local and connector-local test surfaces defined in AGENTS.md instead of inventing a one-off root test layout.
- Downstream proof beads can depend on this convention directly, and the closure audit can consume its artifact bundle without guessing where evidence lives.","notes":"Use this bead to standardize where proof artifacts and rerun entrypoints live before the domain proof beads finalize their own bundles. The goal is not bureaucracy; it is to stop future verification work from scattering transcripts, logs, and scripts across arbitrary locations with inconsistent redaction or naming.

A good exit here should define a small, stable contract: where unit coverage belongs, where integration and e2e scripts belong for crates versus connectors, what a transcript bundle must contain, how structured logs identify scenario, outcome, and redaction state, and how later reviewers invoke the same proof locally or in CI.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:39.357141Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:12:43.378325Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:12:43.378146Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","tooling","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:39.357141Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2337,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"This is the shared proof-contract anchor for 24llg. Downstream verification beads should use it to avoid ad hoc artifact layouts, one-off transcript naming, or inconsistent secret redaction.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:00:26Z"},{"id":2346,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","author":"QuietPine","text":"Implemented the shared verification-bundle contract across fcp-e2e and fwc: added schema_version/layer/scenario_id/artifact_paths/commands metadata, aligned replay commands with rch-backed Cargo reruns, and documented the contract in docs/testing/e2e_log_schema.md plus maintainer references. Local rustfmt check passes on the touched Rust files. Validation is still in progress via rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e --lib. Agent Mail MCP is currently degraded with database errors (`no such table: projects`), so bead comments are serving as the progress log until inbox access recovers.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:02:14Z"},{"id":2347,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","author":"QuietPine","text":"Remote validation via rch reached actual compilation and is currently blocked upstream in the asupersync dependency, not in the evidence-bundle edits. Worker stderr: /dp/asupersync/src/http/h1/listener.rs:493:32 cannot find function payload_to_string in this scope (plus two unused-import warnings in combinator/laws.rs). I’m treating that as an external build blocker for this bead until the dependency tree is green enough to compile fcp-e2e.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:04:58Z"},{"id":2478,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Verified the shared evidence-bundle contract is in place and compiles.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2","title":"[connectors] Graduate explicit stub connectors and fix status drift","description":"Graduate the explicit planned_only/stub connector surfaces and eliminate connector-status drift. The goal is to stop shipping connectors whose handshake, health, invoke, introspection, and manifest surfaces disagree about whether the connector is real. Graduation here means implementing real protocol/auth behavior or intentionally quarantining a connector so the production catalog never overclaims its readiness.","acceptance_criteria":"- Tlon, Zalo, and Zalouser no longer ship as deceptive scaffold connectors; each is either a truthful live MVP slice or an explicit incubating or quarantined connector with machine-readable rationale.
- Manifest, runtime, and catalog status consistency is enforced, including the explicit incubating or quarantined state, and the HuggingFace drift is fixed.
- Connector graduation or quarantine is backed by comprehensive unit tests, connector-level integration coverage, end-to-end CLI or catalog scripts, and detailed structured logs or transcripts.","notes":"This epic has an intentional sequencing rule: 2.1 defines the graduation rubric, 2.6 introduces the explicit incubating or quarantined state and catalog policy, 2.5 locks manifest, runtime, and catalog consistency around that state model, and only then should 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, and 7.3 land concrete connector or generator output changes. That order keeps the truth model stable before touching shipped surfaces.

Do not mark a connector live because its strings changed; require coherent handshake, health, introspection, and invoke behavior plus coverage. Where a connector is not yet worth full implementation, route it through the explicit incubating or quarantined state rather than leaving it as a deceptive shipping scaffold.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:01.226002Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:38:42.181866Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:05:23.988145Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","placeholders","truthfulness","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:01.226002Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2295,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2","author":"Codex","text":"The clever path here is to define one honest graduation standard and reuse it across hand-written connectors, generated connectors, and catalog/status checks.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:01Z"},{"id":2303,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2","author":"Codex","text":"Operational note: treat this bead as a coordination/closure node, not the default claim target. Incubating/Quarantined states added to ConnectorStatus enum.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:05:20Z"},{"id":2370,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2","author":"IvoryHarbor","text":"2026-04-03 progress: validating the Tlon/Zalo/Zalouser closure path exposed that the current workspace still lacked the manifest-backed fwc propagation for connector status/hidden-by-default policy even though the upstream status-model beads were already marked closed. Added focused readiness/search/main tests for hidden-by-default behavior and status metadata.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.1","title":"[connectors] Define a graduation rubric and target capability/auth model for planned_only connectors","description":"Before graduating Tlon, Zalo, and Zalouser, define one honest graduation rubric for planned_only connectors.
